items.title: FA charge Maguire after Man United red card
items.summary: Manchester United defender Harry Maguire has been hit with an FA charge following his red card against Bournemouth.

items.title: Chelsea have highest pre-tax loss in EPL history
items.summary: Chelsea announced pre-tax losses of £262.4 million ($349.1m) for the year ending June 30, 2025 on the same day it was revealed they had spent significantly more on agents' fees than any other English club this season.

items.title: Sophia Wilson rejoins USWNT for Japan matches
items.summary: Forward Sophia Wilson and defender Tierna Davidson will return to the United States women's national team this month for the first time in over a year.

items.title: Zo speelt het Arsenal van Mikel Arteta
items.summary: Arsenal staat op de drempel van de absolute top in het Europese voetbal. Hoofdrol bij deze opmars is er voor Mikel Arteta, een manager die tijdens een paar magere beginjaren op het geduld van de Noord-Londense club mocht rekenen. Geduld dat loont, blijkt nu. Want onder leiding van de Bask is Arsenal uitgegroeid tot de tactisch veelzijdigste topclub van Europa. Longread over de speelwijze van Arteta.

items.title: Götze zet streep onder ophef met fonkelnieuw contract
items.summary: Mario Götze heeft zijn contract bij Eintracht Frankfurt verlengd. De 33-jarige oud-PSV'er ligt nu vast tot medio 2028. Götze is bezig aan zijn vierde seizoen in dienst van de Bundesliga-club.

items.title: From YouTube to real life: why Hashtag United want to go down
items.summary: After a decade of moving up the football pyramid, YouTube team Hashtag United want to be demoted. BBC Sport asks co-founder Spencer Owen: Why?

items.title: Bayern Munich v Manchester United: Women’s Champions League quarter-final, second leg – live
items.summary: ⚽️ Updates from 5.45pm BST kick-off at Allianz Arena ⚽️ United wage bill half of Arsenal’s last season | Email Yara We are now being treated to a lights show inside the stadium as the Bayern players’ names are read out. The stadium looks nearly half full so it’s a decent turnout from the home fans. It’s unseasonably cold here in Munich but that is not dampening the mood here among the 400 or so travelling Manchester United fans, many of whom have enjoyed a stein or two in the old town during the day. The Bayern fans, meanwhile, sounded very optimistic, as they caught the U6 train north to the impressive Allianz Arena. Continue reading...

items.title: 'Oranje moet Japan niet onderschatten op het WK'
items.summary: De interlandperiode van maart zit erop. Nederland speelde met een man minder een moeizame oefenwedstrijd tegen Ecuador. Japan, poulegenoot van Oranje op het WK, won juist knap op Wembley van Engeland (0-1). In Rondje Oranje bespreken verslaggever Martijn Krabbendam en hoofdredacteur Pieter Zwart de tegenstander van Oranje.

items.title: ADO zegt contracten van onder anderen Van Mieghem en Tomas formeel op
items.summary: Blijf via dit blog op de hoogte van alle transfers en transfergeruchten bij en rond clubs in de Eredivisie en de Keuken Kampioen Divisie. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in het buitenland .

items.title: Cambuur siert VI-cover: in de kleedkamer met Henk de Jong
items.summary: De promotie naar de Eredivisie is een feit en daarom siert SC Cambuur de cover van de nieuwste Voetbal International. Op bezoek in Leeuwarden nam trainer Henk de Jong in de kleedkamer een uitvergrote versie in ontvangst uit handen van hoofdredacteur Freek Jansen. De Jong: 'Ik lees de VI mijn hele leven al. Dit is iets speciaals.'

items.title: Montella pictures ‘beautiful path’ as Turkey embrace return to World Cup
items.summary: Italian manager guided his team past Kosovo in their playoff final, with Kenan Yildiz a burgeoning superstar Vincenzo Montella was mid-answer when a side door burst open and more than a dozen Turkey players and staff stormed his press conference, showering him with liquid. Within seconds he was bouncing up and down among them, the flimsy advertising banner behind him wobbling furiously. He had already been applauded into the room by travelling media for achieving what nobody in the previous two decades could: guiding one of football’s great enigmas to a World Cup and setting millions of hearts alight. Dark horses? That will be the time-honoured joke about Turkey this summer given their propensity at recent European Championship appearances to fall short of their excitable billing. A quarter-final finish at Euro 2024 at least constituted progress and Montella, whose team did a professional job in spoiling what had been set up as a wild party for Kosovo, has the chance to back it up. Continue reading...

items.title: Football Daily | Italy blow another World Cup chance. Will they ever learn?
items.summary: Sign up now! Sign up now! Sign up now? Sign up now! Watching 10-man Italy blow their last chance to qualify for this summer’s Geopolitics World Cup, Football Daily couldn’t help but remember the schoolmasterly ticking-off with which the legendary commentator Barry Davies admonished a similarly depleted team of their predecessors 24 years ago. That particular iteration of Italy had at least made it to the summer jamboree, but upon seeing them being dumped out in the last 16 by co-hosts South Korea at Daejeon World Cup Stadium, Davies said what the rest of us were thinking: “And the Italians are out because they never learn,” he clucked. Of course, it could also be argued the Italians were out because they were the victims of what could charitably described as a series of farcical refereeing decisions, but their natural inclination towards extreme caution certainly didn’t help. It’s the best night of my life and I’ve had some wonderful nights, you know, stories with Östersund and European adventures and [Bigger Cup], Premier League wins, but this just feels like more. You can feel the atmosphere in the ground. It’s very rarely you go to football places and feel that. I think when you’re a national team … you’re working for more than yourself, so when you add all that up and then put the context of the game and the environment and the atmosphere, it was just a special moment” – he’s not had the easiest couple of years so Graham Potter was careful to make sure he fully enjoyed basking in the glow of success after Sweden’s thrilling 3-2 GWC playoff win over Poland. The game against Japan showed how useless England are. The unrealistic expectations are unbelievable!!!” – Jeff Sax. Not sure Fifa suit Arsène Wenger’s suggestion that daylight must be between the attacker and defender (yesterday’s Football Daily Off Course section) is going to be helpful resolving offside decisions in all situations. What about evening matches? Will refs have to carry torches now, or perhaps one of those seasonal affective disorder light panels? Or is VAR planning to utilise pitch-side laser beam daylight analysis, which is likely to further increase decision deliberations to their existing infinite Matrix Time ponderings? And which might lead to possible permanent blindness for those persistently diving forwards who roll around on the ground just a little too long to avoid Fifa-sanctioned laser-frazzled retinas. Although granted some free laser-eye surgery might benefit one or two number nines I can think of. And what about those 5.30pm kick-off twilight zone offsides? Blimey. Thanks, Arsène” – Steve Malone Thank you for the story on how Vindaloo was created – I must admit, I hadn’t realised the connection with Vindaloo and Waterloo, but it is very clever. Scotland’s Colourbox’s fantastic Official Colourbox World Cup Theme from ‘86 immediately came to mind as well for this category. Famous for being rejected by both BBC and ITV, I first heard it on a 4AD compilation album, and I can’t wait to blast it out in June! It just needs Alan Partridge previewing the tournament with this soundtrack” – Tom Pratt. Being one of the authors of Monday’s ‘four superb letters’, I would like to announce that being inducted into Andy Morrison’s fanclub is better than a prizeless letter o’ the day, which I have won a few times!” – Nigel Sanders. When Brighton visit Spurs on 18 April, will it be called Ze Derbi?” – Phil Taverner. This is an extract from our daily football email … Football Daily. To get the full version, just visit this page and follow the instructions. Continue reading...

items.title: Ranked: All 48 teams that can win the 2026 World Cup
items.summary: We have the final 48 teams that can win the FIFA World Cup this summer. Let's go through them all and rank where they stand.

items.title: One thing to know about each World Cup team
items.summary: The World Cup lineup is complete as 48 teams are confirmed. What's one thing you should know about each?

items.title: Lamine Yamal condemns racist abuse aimed at Egypt
items.summary: Lamine Yamal says racist chants heard during Spain's 0-0 draw with Egypt in Barcelona on Tuesday were "disrespectful and intolerable".

items.title: Chelsea make pre-tax loss of £262.4m - biggest in Premier League history
items.summary: Chelsea announce a Premier League‑record pre‑tax loss of £262m for the 2024‑25 season.

items.title: On the plane or the sofa? How England’s 2026 World Cup squad is shaping up
items.summary: Only half of the 26 places appear nailed-on and some players benefited from missing the Uruguay and Japan games Jordan Pickford remains the undisputed No 1. Harry Kane is irreplaceable up front. Declan Rice and Elliot Anderson look certain to start in midfield, nobody has emerged as a realistic challenger to Bukayo Saka on the right and Jude Bellingham’s hopes of grabbing the No 10 spot were done a world of good by other challengers failing to impress against Japan and Uruguay. Continue reading...

items.title: Who were Scotland's winners and losers from international break?
items.summary: After a friendly defeats by Japan and Ivory Coast, which Scotland players are the winners and losers of the international break?

items.title: BBC Sport's McNulty picks his England World Cup squad - do you agree?
items.summary: BBC Sport chief football writer Phil McNulty picks his 26-man England World Cup squad - do you agree?

items.title: World Cup 2026: which countries have qualified and how did they do it?
items.summary: We now know all 48 teams that will play in the US, Canada and Mexico later this year – this is how they got there Nine countries qualified as group winners – Egypt, Morocco, Tunisia, Algeria, Ghana, Cape Verde, South Africa, Senegal and Côte d’Ivoire – and the Democratic Republic of Congo gave the continent a 10th representative at the tournament by battling through the playoffs. Egypt Mohamed Salah scored twice as Hossam Hassan’s side beat Djibouti 3-0 in Casablanca in October and made up for missing out on Qatar 2022 by reaching the finals with a game in hand. This will be Egypt’s fourth finals, even though they have yet to win a game. Bizarrely, the Pharaohs did qualify for the first World Cup, in 1930, but missed their boat from Marseille to South America after a storm delayed them. Continue reading...

items.title: "C'est non" : l'ACFF bloque la réforme de la Pro League !
items.summary: L'aile francophone de l'Union belge de football, l'ACFF, réagit de manière critique à la récente décision de la Pro League concernant les équipes U23. L'administrateur délégué, Benjamin Vasseur, prend acte du vote mais souligne que la logique sportive doit rester la priorité.

items.title: Hallucinant! Chelsea boekt grootste verlies ooit in de Premier League: belachelijk hoge cijfers
items.summary: Chelsea heeft een stevig negatief record te pakken in de Premier League. Volgens BBC noteerde de Londense club een verlies van maar liefst 301 miljoen euro over het seizoen 2024/25.

items.title: Passé à côté de son sujet : Loïs Openda a-t-il grillé sa dernière cartouche chez les Diables Rouges ?
items.summary: Romelu Lukaku ayant manqué les huit derniers matchs des Diables, Loïs Openda avait une belle occasion à saisir pour récupérer la place de numéro 2 derrière Charles De Ketelaere, et peut-être de titulaire si Big Rom' est absent au Mondial. L'attaquant de la Juventus n'en a pas du tout fait bon usage.

items.title: Hier is de nieuwe FIFA-ranking: hier staat België en een nieuwe nummer 1
items.summary: De FIFA-ranking is woensdag bijgewerkt na de laatste barragewedstrijden voor het WK. België blijft op de 9e plaats.

items.title: Maxim De Cuyper, een van de grote verliezers van de interlandbreak?
items.summary: Titularis tijdens de oefenwedstrijden van de Rode Duivels in de Verenigde Staten heeft Maxim De Cuyper niet echt kunnen overtuigen. Dat doet vragen rijzen over de positie van linksachter bij de nationale ploeg.

items.title: "Un manque de respect" : Lamine Yamal réagit au scandale des chants racistes en Espagne
items.summary: Lamine Yamal a réagi dans une publication Instagram aux chants anti-musulmans de supporters espagnols lors du match nul entre l'Espagne et l'Égypte (0-0).

items.title: Anderlecht récupère un international en pleine confiance : véritable homme fort de son équipe
items.summary: Le RSC Anderlecht pourra de nouveau compter sur Mihajlo Cvetković cette semaine. Le jeune attaquant revient avec un maximum de confiance après un but très important inscrit avec la Serbie U19.

items.title: Mag deze Rode Duivel een kruis over het WK maken? Laatste kans verspeeld
items.summary: Doordat Romelu Lukaku de laatste acht wedstrijden van de Duivels miste, had Loïs Openda een uitgelezen kans om de plek als nummer twee achter Charles De Ketelaere te pakken, en misschien zelfs basisspeler te worden als Big Rom' het WK mist. De spits van Juventus heeft die kans echter totaal niet gegrepen.

items.title: 📷 'Belgische voetbalbond in crisis: miljoenenschuld heeft gevolgen voor spelers'
items.summary: Het gaat niet goed met de financiën van de Belgische voetbalbond. Dat meldt transferjournalist Sacha Tavolieri op zijn sociale media.

items.title: Un ancien Diable Rouge inquiet pour Romelu Lukaku et la Belgique : "Ça devient utopique d'imaginer cela"
items.summary: Les inquiétudes autour de Romelu Lukaku grandissent à l'approche de la Coupe du monde. L'ancien Diable Rouge Marc Degryse se montre inquiet.
